evidence_quotes.quote: The arbitrary removal of these games by publishers deprives consumers of content for which they have paid and for which they have often invested considerable time and passion.

paragraphs.text: Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to you to draw your attention to a crucial issue concerning the protection of consumers of online video games. I would like to propose that you incorporate specific protections for video games under the Digital Fairness Act. Online video games are a major form of entertainment and digital art. The arbitrary removal of these games by publishers deprives consumers of content for which they have paid and for which they have often invested considerable time and passion. This practice raises major concerns about consumer rights and the preservation of our digital cultural heritage. It is important to note that this initiative enjoys massive support, as demonstrated by the Stop Killing Games campaign, which has collected more than 1.2 million signatures across the European Union. This level of public support underlines the urgency and relevance of this protection for players. Currently, the Digital Fairness Act aims to protect consumers from unfair practices online. Incorporating protections for video games would further strengthen this text and ensure that video game consumers also benefit from legal protection. Such a measure would be beneficial not only for consumers, but also for the video game industry in general, by encouraging a relationship of trust between consumers and publishers. I strongly encourage you to support this initiative and work towards its integration into the Digital Fairness Act. I am convinced that this step is essential to meet the expectations and needs of players across Europe. Cordially, Robin Milla.
